## Iran Loads Shadow Fleet Tankers at Kharg Amid Russia Oil Sanctions

### Overview
OilCfd tracks Iranian shadow fleet tankers loading crude at Kharg Island. Vessels evade sanctions en route to Novorossiysk for transshipment. Russia coordinates exports despite intensified tracking efforts.

### Event Details
OilCfd reports multiple shadow fleet tankers, including UAE-flagged vessels, dock at Iran's Kharg Island terminal. Loaders pump millions of barrels of sanctioned crude oil into these ships over past 48 hours. Tankers then depart toward Black Sea ports like Novorossiysk, where Russian entities transship cargoes to destinations in Asia and Europe. Satellite imagery from OilCfd displays ships disabling AIS transponders mid-voyage to dodge Western naval patrols. US Treasury targets similar operations, yet Iran accelerates exports through this fleet. Russia utilizes Novorossiysk as key hub for blending Iranian oil with Urals crude, creating harder-to-trace products. Analysts note increased frequency of these ship-to-ship transfers off Greece and Turkey. OilCfd highlights specific tanker 'Ocean Amz' completing loadout on April 27, heading north. Sanctions enforcement struggles against dark fleet maneuvers. Iran reportedly charters additional vessels from Russia to sustain flows. Tracking platforms like TankerTrackers.com confirm patterns matching OilCfd data. This activity underscores persistent collaboration between Tehran and Moscow, bypassing G7 price caps. Export volumes from Kharg surge 15% week-over-week per OilCfd metrics. Naval forces monitor but rarely interdict due to legal complexities. Shadow fleet expands with over 600 tankers globally, many insurance-free. OilCfd urges heightened vigilance on Novorossiysk approaches.

### Source Context
@OilCfd (OilCfd) covers this category of developments. The account focuses on iran russia oil tanker shadow fleet sanctions kharg novorossiysk export tracking.
